input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the

data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they want a reference guide. This is a knowledge/guidance question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a comprehensive answer directly.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS development)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Version discovery:** Check [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the minimum supported Xcode version.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS that supports MAUI.
- Use the `.pkg` installer for macOS (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Version discovery:**
  ```
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install .NET MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- Verify with:
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```

## 4. Install Android SDK (via command-line tools or Android Studio)

- **Option A (recommended):** Install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) — it bundles the SDK, emulator, and SDK Manager.
- **Option B (CLI-only):** Download [command-line tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only) and use `sdkmanager`.
- Required components:
  ```
  sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  ```
- **Version discovery:** Check `dotnet workload` output or `$(AndroidSdkDirectory)` in build logs for the target API level.

## 5. Set environment variables

Add to `~/.zshrc`:
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
export JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home"
```

## 6. Accept Android licenses

```bash
sdkmanager --licenses
```

## 7. Create an Android emulator

```bash
avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_API_34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

## 8. Verify everything works

```bash
# Check .NET and workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Check Xcode
xcodebuild -version

# Check Android
adb --version
emulator -list-avds

# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 9. Run on targets

```bash
# iOS Simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Android Emulator (start emulator first)
emulator @Pixel_API_34 &
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

---

**Key version-compatibility reference:**  
Always check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) — it lists exact Xcode, Android SDK, and .NET SDK version requirements for each MAUI release.
